decisions.title: Prioritize Canvas V2 GA readiness over incremental version improvements
decisions.context: ENG-9441 focuses on Canvas version improvements. With Canvas as a core 'Embed Deeper' product, we need to decide if incremental improvements serve GA readiness or if we should batch these for post-launch polish.

arguments.for: Incremental improvements reduce technical debt and improve user experience before wider adoption. Version management is foundational to Canvas's value prop.
arguments.against: Resources could accelerate GA-blocking features. Version improvements can be iterated post-launch based on real usage patterns.

decisions.title: Standardize infrastructure migration approach across remaining environments
decisions.context: Multiple parallel kluctl migrations (ENG-7781, ENG-10088-90, ENG-5980) and infrastructure consolidation tasks (ENG-7722-35) are in flight. Need unified completion strategy to avoid resource fragmentation.

arguments.for: Unified approach reduces context switching, enables knowledge sharing, and accelerates completion. Infrastructure stability is moat-building work.
arguments.against: Teams have different timelines and dependencies. Forcing synchronization could slow down teams that are ready to move faster.

decisions.title: Accelerate Sift Edge production readiness vs continuing benchmarking
decisions.context: ENG-10530 focuses on Edge benchmarking and stress testing. Edge is a 2026 key product ('Build the Moat'). Need to decide if current benchmarking is sufficient for limited production rollout or if we need more comprehensive testing.
decisions.blockedBy: Benchmarking completion and performance baseline definition

arguments.for: Limited production rollout with real customer data will provide better signals than synthetic benchmarks. Speed to market builds competitive moat.
arguments.against: Insufficient testing could damage customer trust and brand. Edge's value prop depends on reliability. Production issues are costly to remediate.

decisions.title: Resource allocation: Security compliance vs product feature velocity
decisions.context: Multiple security/compliance tasks (ENG-9320, ENG-9308, ENG-9321, ENG-10617, ENG-9161) competing with feature work. These are 'Build the Moat' investments but may slow 'Nail the Core' velocity.

arguments.for: Security and compliance are table stakes for enterprise customers. Incidents or audit failures are existential risks. These build trust moat.
arguments.against: Customers buy product value first. Security theater without product-market fit wastes resources. Can leverage security as a forcing function for architectural improvements.

decisions.title: Define data volume limits strategy: hard limits vs progressive degradation
decisions.context: ENG-9568 and ENG-10436 address data volume management. Critical for 'Nail the Core' - system reliability under customer scale. Need architectural decision on constraint enforcement.
decisions.blockedBy: Customer usage analysis and cost modeling

arguments.for: Hard limits provide predictable costs and performance. Clear boundaries simplify capacity planning and prevent runaway resource consumption.
arguments.against: Progressive degradation (rate limiting, sampling) provides better customer experience. Allows graceful handling of spikes and enables upsell conversations vs hard stops.

timeAudit.weekSummary: Heavy infrastructure migration coordination consuming leadership bandwidth. Security compliance work creating cross-team dependencies. Product teams need clearer prioritization signals.
alerts: 63% of engineering capacity on infrastructure vs product features this week
Canvas and Edge both at risk: need executive decision on resource allocation
4 decision points requiring input by Friday - schedule dedicated decision-making block
Infrastructure migrations creating meeting overload (22 this week) - consolidate standup cadence

events.title: Security/Compliance Resource Allocation Decision
events.description: Finalize Q2 resource split between security compliance work and product feature velocity. Impacts 5+ eng teams.

events.title: Sift Edge Production Readiness Gate
events.description: Decision: Proceed with limited production rollout or continue benchmarking. Benchmarking results review + customer pilot readiness assessment.

events.title: Canvas Version Strategy Review
events.description: Prioritization decision: GA-blocking features vs incremental version improvements. Align with Canvas launch timeline.

events.title: Data Volume Limits Architecture Decision
events.description: Hard limits vs progressive degradation strategy. Requires customer usage analysis and cost modeling completion.

events.title: Infrastructure Migration Standup Consolidation
events.description: New unified standup for all kluctl migrations (dev, tec, ula, lockheed, gov). Reduce coordination overhead from 8 to 2 meetings/week.

events.title: Infrastructure Migration Strategy Alignment
events.description: Unified completion strategy for remaining environment migrations. Define phasing, resource allocation, and completion targets for Q2.

events.title: Channel Search Replica Production Enablement
events.description: ENG-8521 completion target. Performance validation and rollout plan approval. Critical for 'Nail the Core' system reliability.

events.title: Security Compliance Milestone: SOC2 Readiness
events.description: Incident response, disaster recovery, and change management docs complete. Hardened container images deployed to prod. Secrets re-encryption complete.

events.title: Q2 Strategy Review: Pillar Health Check
events.description: Review North Star metric progress and pillar status. Canvas and Edge both at risk - assess course correction needs for H1 targets.

events.title: sift-stack-py Release (CHANGELOG Update)
events.description: ENG-10279 completion. Python SDK release supporting new Canvas capabilities and Edge integration patterns.

events.title: Canvas GA Launch Target
events.description: Tentative GA date pending version improvements and data volume limits decisions. Critical 'Embed Deeper' pillar deliverable.

events.title: Sift Edge Limited Production Rollout
events.description: Target for first 5 production customer deployments post-benchmarking. 'Build the Moat' flagship initiative for H2.

events.title: Infrastructure Modernization Complete
events.description: All environments migrated to kluctl, legacy systems decommissioned. Frees engineering capacity for product velocity in H2.
